column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-scenes-windows (sw-01 … sw-13).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard -> runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Structural rules grep
# cannot express (containment: openWindow/openSettings/SettingsLink INSIDE a MenuBarExtra closure) live in
# ast-grep/*.yml (tier 2). sw-09 (the id no-op) has NO standalone rule — it needs the project-wide
# scene-id <-> open-call cross-reference the agent builds in READ; the sw-09 tell below only surfaces the
# openWindow(id:)/dismissWindow(id:) call SITES so the agent can cross-check them against scene ids.

column4: STALE/INVENTED scene API → Settings {} / SettingsLink / openSettings (Preferences{}, show*Window: selectors); @FocusedDocument is NOT real → custom FocusedValues key; DocumentGroupLaunchScene is macOS-ABSENT (iOS/iPadOS/Mac Catalyst/visionOS only)
Settings/Preferences reached via NavigationLink/.sheet — if there is no Settings {} scene, ⌘, is dead → add the Settings {} scene (sw-02). READ the App body to confirm.
Save/Apply/Cancel button — if inside a Settings/Preferences Form, that is a Windows-style modal; macOS Settings is modeless, no Save/Cancel (sw-03 HIG). READ to confirm it is the settings surface.
a .sheet — if it stands in for a separate window (inspector, 2nd document), use a scene + openWindow, not a modal sheet trapped in this window (sw-04)
AppKit status item in a SwiftUI app → use a MenuBarExtra scene (sw-05); whether to bridge at all = cross_ref appkit-overuse

a scene declaration — confirm it has .defaultSize AND .windowResizability (scene-modifier sizing layer); missing both = uncontrolled Mac window (sw-08). content-frame sizing = cross_ref layout-and-tables

open/dismiss-by-id CALL SITE — cross-check the id STRING against every registered Window(id:)/WindowGroup(id:)/UtilityWindow(id:) scene in the project; an unmatched id is a SILENT no-op (sw-09). REPORT only if no scene registers that string.
openWindow in use — confirm a matching @Environment(\.dismissWindow) close path exists for any auxiliary window (sw-10); dismissWindow is macOS 14+
an App type — if it has only scenes and needs quit-on-last-window/launch/terminate, it needs @NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor (sw-11). READ: is there an adaptor?
window chrome — confirm .windowStyle matches the design (e.g. .hiddenTitleBar for content-forward); absent/wrong style = sw-12 (case strings UNVERIFIED)
navigationTitle — if placed INSIDE a Window scene it REPLACES the titlebar text (sw-13); structural nav migration = cross_ref navigation-toolbars
